---
title: "Bayesian Statistics — Overview"
author: "Johnny van Doorn"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
---

The slides are on 1 continuous page. To get a pdf, I recommend you use the print option in your browser to print the html to pdf (open the slides and press `ctrl + p` (Windows) or `cmd + p` (MacOS)).

**Textbook:** Johnson, A. A., Ott, M. Q., & Dogucu, M. (2022). *Bayes Rules! An Introduction to Applied Bayesian Modeling*. Chapman & Hall / CRC. Free online: <https://www.bayesrulesbook.com>.

## Slides — 2026

| Date | Lecture | Topics | Slides |
|---|---|---|---|
| 8 Jun  | **Lecture 4** — Conjugate Models & Choosing Your Prior        | Conjugacy; selecting plausible priors; prior predictive check (Ch. 5)                                          | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture4_Conjugacy/lecture4_conjugacy.html) |
| 10 Jun | **Lecture 5** — Obtaining the Posterior: Grid Approximation & MCMC | Why we need numerical methods; grid approximation; the intuition behind MCMC; convergence diagnostics (Ch. 6 & 7) | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture5_GridMCMC/lecture5_gridmcmc.html) |
| 15 Jun | **Lecture 6a** — Posterior Prediction & Hypothesis Specification | Prior + posterior predictive; predictive p-values; hypothesis testing as model comparison; ESP running example  | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture6_PredictionReporting/lecture6_predictionreporting.html) |
| 15 Jun | **Lecture 6b** — Using the Posterior: Inference, Prediction & Hypothesis Testing | Point estimates; equal-tailed & HPD credible intervals; interval hypotheses via Bayes factors; point hypotheses via Savage–Dickey; Dutch Railways running example (Ch. 8) | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture6b_PosteriorInference/lecture6b_posteriorinference.html) |
| 17 Jun | **Lecture 7** — Reporting Guidelines & Exam Preparation       | van Doorn et al. (2021) reporting checklist; five common exam mistakes; error-spotting exercises; mock exam     | [link](https://johnnydoorn.github.io/teaching-statistics/bayesian-statistics-pml/2026/Lecture7_ExamPrep/lecture7_examprep.html) |

## Contact

For questions, errata or suggestions, email Johnny van Doorn at [j.b.vandoorn@uva.nl](mailto:j.b.vandoorn@uva.nl).
